title: xeraea riedelii
intro: Xeraea riedelii, also known as the "Kawa Kawa" plant, is a type of evergreen vine native to the Pacific Islands, specifically the island of Rarotonga in the Cook Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a stimulant and to treat various health conditions. The leaves of the Xeraea riedelii plant have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain, inflammation, and improve overall health. Xeraea riedelii can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other sleep disorders.
